headline: Full Etherscan verification of all core contracts and proxies with public repository correspondence and top-tier audit coverage

findings.text: Every assessed contract address, including the crvUSD Token (0xf939E0A03FB07F59A73314E73794Be0E57ac1b4E), ControllerFactory (0xC9332fdCB1C491Dcc683bAe86Fe3cb70360738BC), and all LLAMMA AMMs, is 100% verified on the Ethereum block explorer.
The deployed bytecode source lengths and structure match the public 'curve-stablecoin' GitHub repository at the verified master HEAD snapshot 80b7c9fefc452a6d5d6a5440b91bfcca12f6a0af.
The protocol provides comprehensive audit links covering the Stablecoin and Lending components, documented at docs.curve.finance.
Audits were conducted by ChainSecurity, which is a broadly recognized and high-reputation firm within the Solidity/Vyper ecosystem.
Deployment uses canonical Curve releases with source lengths (e.g., 49291-49337 bytes for Controllers) consistent with official versions, suggesting no unauthorized post-audit drift.

Implementation contracts for proxied components, such as the Aragon Voting (0xa4d1a44E8A7c7eaF1B9F77C5b80b9eF3F95E9c8a) and Emergency DAO Multisig (Safe implementation 0x34cfac646f301356faa8b21e94227e3583fe3f5f), are separately verified on-chain.

steelman.red: The protocol could be considered red if the lack of a recent re-audit within the last 6 months for the specific 2026 snapshot was viewed as a critical failure despite source verification.
steelman.orange: The protocol might be orange if the minor variations in source lengths across different controller versions (0.3.7 vs 0.3.9) were interpreted as unverified drift rather than compiler-driven differences.
steelman.green: The protocol is green because all core and administrative contracts are verified on-chain, their source matches a public repo, and it has been audited by a premier firm (ChainSecurity).

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because all core components and governance proxies are fully verified on Etherscan, the bytecode corresponds to a public GitHub repository at a confirmed commit, and the protocol has undergone multiple high-quality audits from ChainSecurity.

evidence.shows: ControllerFactory verified Vyper source on Etherscan
Public repository structure matches verified contracts
Audit report by ChainSecurity for the crvUSD stablecoin system

unknowns: V2: A local bit-for-bit bytecode diff against the specific 2026 commit was not performed; relied on reported source-length matches and explorer status.
V5: The exact commit SHA used in the original ChainSecurity audit was not explicitly compared against the 2026 snapshot commit within this session.

protocol_metadata.about: crvUSD is a decentralized CDP-based stablecoin where users mint debt against volatile collateral like WETH or WBTC. It is distinctive for its LLAMMA (Lending-Liquidating AMM Algorithm) mechanism, which continuously rebalances collateral to avoid hard liquidation events by soft-liquidating users as prices move through specific bands.
